req.title: Transfer funds between own accounts (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.
preconditions: source.owner == target.owner

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-053 defines a daily transfer cap of $25,000 for intra-customer transfers. The key equivalence partitions are: (1) valid transfer within limit, (2) transfer exceeding daily limit, (3) transfer with insufficient funds, and (4) unauthorised actor. Boundary values sit at $24,999.99 (below), $25,000.00 (at), and $25,000.01 (above). State transitions include account balance moving from sufficient to zero/negative and the daily-limit counter crossing the threshold. A decision table covers combinations of ownership, balance, and daily-limit state. Tests are designed to be idempotent by using fixture-managed accounts that are reset between runs.

tests.name: test_transfer_valid_amount_within_daily_limit
preconditions: Authenticated customer session exists
source account owned by customer with balance >= $1,000.00
target account owned by same customer
daily transfer total for customer is $0.00
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=$500.00, source and target account IDs
trace: R-FIN-053 positive path: amount well within daily limit
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_valid_amount_within_daily_limit(api_client, funded_account_pair):
    src, tgt = funded_account_pair
    src_balance_before = <get_balance>(api_client, src)
    tgt_balance_before = <get_balance>(api_client, tgt)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "500.00", "currency": "USD"}
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert <get_balance>(api_client, src) == src_balance_before - 500.00
    assert <get_balance>(api_client, tgt) == tgt_balance_before + 500.00

tests.name: test_transfer_exceeds_daily_limit_returns_422
preconditions: Authenticated customer session exists
source account owned by customer with balance >= $30,000.00
target account owned by same customer
daily transfer total for customer is $0.00
expected: response body error code indicates daily limit exceeded
trace: R-FIN-053 error pathway: 422 when daily limit exceeded
Negative Path: single transfer above $25,000 cap
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_exceeds_daily_limit_returns_422(api_client, funded_account_pair):
    src, tgt = funded_account_pair
    src_balance_before = <get_balance>(api_client, src)
    tgt_balance_before = <get_balance>(api_client, tgt)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "25000.01", "currency": "USD"}
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert "limit" in body["error"].lower()
    assert <get_balance>(api_client, src) == src_balance_before
    assert <get_balance>(api_client, tgt) == tgt_balance_before

tests.name: test_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Authenticated customer session exists
source account owned by customer with balance >= $25,000.00
target account owned by same customer
daily transfer total for customer is $0.00
trace: R-FIN-053 boundary: transfer exactly at the $25,000 daily cap must be accepted
Boundary Value Analysis: on-point at upper limit
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit_boundary_accepted(api_client, funded_account_pair):
    src, tgt = funded_account_pair
    src_before = <get_balance>(api_client, src)
    tgt_before = <get_balance>(api_client, tgt)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "25000.00", "currency": "USD"}
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"On-point limit rejected: {resp.status_code}"
    assert "transfer_id" in resp.json()
    assert <get_balance>(api_client, src) == src_before - 25000.00
    assert <get_balance>(api_client, tgt) == tgt_before + 25000.00

tests.name: test_transfer_one_cent_above_daily_limit_boundary_rejected
preconditions: Authenticated customer session exists
source account owned by customer with balance >= $25,000.01
target account owned by same customer
daily transfer total for customer is $0.00
expected: error message references daily transfer limit
no balance change on either account
trace: R-FIN-053 boundary: one cent above cap must be declined with 422
Boundary Value Analysis: off-point just above upper limit
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_one_cent_above_daily_limit_boundary_rejected(api_client, funded_account_pair):
    src, tgt = funded_account_pair
    src_before = <get_balance>(api_client, src)
    tgt_before = <get_balance>(api_client, tgt)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "25000.01", "currency": "USD"}
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Off-point limit accepted: {resp.status_code}"
    assert <get_balance>(api_client, src) == src_before
    assert <get_balance>(api_client, tgt) == tgt_before

tests.name: test_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Authenticated customer session exists
source account owned by customer with balance = $100.00
target account owned by same customer
daily transfer total for customer is $0.00
expected: response body error code indicates insufficient funds
trace: R-FIN-053 error pathway: 409 on insufficient funds
Negative Path: transfer amount exceeds available balance
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409(api_client, low_balance_account_pair):
    src, tgt = low_balance_account_pair
    src_before = <get_balance>(api_client, src)
    tgt_before = <get_balance>(api_client, tgt)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "500.00", "currency": "USD"}
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 409, f"Expected 409, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "insufficient" in body.get("error", "").lower()
    assert <get_balance>(api_client, src) == src_before
    assert <get_balance>(api_client, tgt) == tgt_before

tests.name: test_transfer_unauthorised_customer_returns_403
preconditions: Two customers A and B exist with separate accounts
customer_b_session is authenticated
source and target accounts belong to customer A
actions: POST /transfers/internal using customer B session with customer A source and target account IDs, amount=$100.00
trace: R-FIN-053 precondition: source.owner == target.owner must be enforced
Decision Table: row where actor != account owner -> deny
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_unauthorised_customer_returns_403(api_client_b, owned_account_pair_a):
    src, tgt = owned_account_pair_a
    src_before = <get_balance_admin>(src)
    payload = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "100.00", "currency": "USD"}
    resp = api_client_b.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 403), f"Expected 401/403, got {resp.status_code}"
    assert <get_balance_admin>(src) == src_before

tests.name: test_cumulative_transfers_crossing_daily_limit_second_declined
preconditions: Authenticated customer session exists
source account owned by customer with balance >= $25,100.00
target account owned by same customer
daily transfer total for customer is $0.00
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=$0.02 (should push total to $25,000.01 and be declined)
expected: Second transfer: HTTP 422 with daily limit error
Source balance decremented only by $24,999.99 in total
trace: R-FIN-053: daily limit is cumulative across multiple transfers
State Transition Coverage: state moves from under-limit to over-limit mid-session
tests.executable:
def test_cumulative_transfers_crossing_daily_limit_second_declined(api_client, funded_account_pair):
    src, tgt = funded_account_pair
    src_before = <get_balance>(api_client, src)
    p1 = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "24999.99", "currency": "USD"}
    r1 = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=p1)
    assert r1.status_code in (200, 201), f"First transfer failed: {r1.status_code}"
    p2 = {"source_account_id": src, "target_account_id": tgt, "amount": "0.02", "currency": "USD"}
    r2 = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=p2)
    assert r2.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 for limit breach, got {r2.status_code}"
    assert "limit" in r2.json().get("error", "").lower()
    assert <get_balance>(api_client, src) == src_before - 24999.99
